purpose: Learned state for the paper-trading system. The daily trader READS this before trading. The weekly review WRITES to it. Bounded self-adjustment only.

adjustment_limits.note: The review may pause a source (multiplier 0) or scale it up to 2x base size.

macro_rules.risk_off_definition: VIX > 25 OR major war/escalation OR circuit-breaker OR Fed emergency

quality_gate.philosophy: Buffett-style: only buy businesses, not tickers.

investment_committee.purpose: Six lenses, one-line votes per member. Net-positive required. Munger pure-hype rejection counts double.

members.Peter Lynch: Understand in one sentence? Growth reasonable for price?

overfitting_guard.rule: No multiplier change justified by <5 graded samples. If graded sample count <30, conclusions labeled LOW CONFIDENCE.

operational_details.corporate_action_check: Check news before >30% drops; adjust instead of sell if split/dividend
operational_details.signal_dedupe: Keep processed_signals fingerprints; never act twice on same disclosure
operational_details.dynamic_sizing: Base buy = 2% of current total portfolio value
operational_details.instrument_filter: Common stocks only; broad ETFs allowed
operational_details.earnings_proximity: Defer buys if earnings within 3 trading days

Week 2 Close (Friday). CRITICAL: Portfolio underwater; major underperformance vs SPY. Repriced all positions at 2026-07-18 close. AAPL 333.74 (+8.21% from cost 308.63), AXP 355.35 (+0.96%), KO 81.56 (-3.10%), SPY 743.29 (-1.27% from cost 752.6219), MTUM 302.09 (-7.07% from cost 325.0506). MACRO: NORMAL → BORDERLINE (VIX 18.77 up from 15.87, SPY 743.29 down -1.01% from 753.98 day prior, but still above 200-DMA ~745). Portfolio: $97,824.82 (-2.18% from $100k start). SPY: $99,888.80 (-0.11% from start). **Underperformance: -206bp**. Root causes: (1) Cash drag: 22.6% uninvested post-V2 migration amplifies losses in down market. (2) MTUM collapse: -7.07% momentum factor unwinds on sector rotation away from growth/momentum names. (3) Berkshire mixed: AAPL recovery +8.21% insufficient to offset MTUM drag and cash drag. Allocation check: CORE_SPY 48.4% (target 50%, drift 1.6pp), MTUM 22.8% (target 25%, drift 2.2pp, approaching rebalance consideration). VIX 18.77 between 15 and 20, stays at 25% vol-scale target (no bucket change). All positions >-20% stops (19-23% room). Charter: peak $102,021.29; current drawdown -4.28%, within -15% pain limit but directional concern rising. Key insight: V2 migration was *correct design* (momentum overhyped cycle), but execution timing *wrong* (bought MTUM at peak); should have staggered or waited for VIX >20 bucket. Lessons: (1) VIX-scaled allocation is sound but requires initial VIX entry discipline. (2) Broadly: concentration in 3 Berkshire names + overweight cash is bottleneck. (3) Congress shadow-tracking still blocked; no data to grade. Macro accuracy: 16 right / 0 wrong on NORMAL calls (conservative & correct). No new trades executed (no new 13F or insider candidates). Grading note: None of initial 3 Berkshire trades hit 14-day window yet (first window closes 2026-07-20, two days away; AXP earnings 2026-07-24 within 6 days, monitor for earnings drift). Confidence on V2 strategy design: HIGH. Confidence on execution/timing: LOW (cash drag + MTUM entry timing). Next actions: (1) Monday 2026-07-21: MANDATORY manager resolution (Pershing, Scion, Duquesne, Appaloosa) + insider re-scan. (2) Assess MTUM rebalance options if VIX >20 or drift >5pp. (3) Consider emergency re-allocation if chart breaches -15% pain limit (distance: 10.87pp, about -1,087 portfolio value). (4) Evaluate cash deployment strategy for insider cluster opportunities if candidates appear. Simulated portfolio - no real money.
